book_summary: 1 Peter 1-5 has full chapter coverage with source-audited-draft restored translation and mystical companion layers. The book preserves sojourner identity, living hope, holiness, sibling-love, living stones/royal priesthood, honorable conduct, difficult household texts with anti-abuse and anti-slavery safeguards, hope defended with gentleness, gift-service, non-domineering shepherding, anxiety cast upon Divine care, and restoration by the Divine of all grace.
translation_notes: Sojourner/exile language should not erase Jewish diaspora context.
1 Peter 2-3 household suffering passages must not be used to sanctify slavery, abuse, coercive labor, or passive endurance of harm.
Apologia is defense/explanation of hope with gentleness and reverence, not combative apologetics.
Elders shepherd without lording over; non-domination is explicit.
The adversarial lion image is vigilance language, not permission to treat people as enemies.
source_witness_audit_flags: 1 Peter 2:18-25 and 3:1-7 require reception-history and pastoral abuse-safeguard audit.
1 Peter 3:19-21 spirits in prison/baptism requires second-pass source and tradition audit.
1 Peter 4:6 good news to dead ones requires second-pass audit.
